title: Alang Fort
tagline: Alang Fort — heritage city of Maharashtra

overview.short: Alang Fort (also known as Alangad or Alang) is a fort located in the Kalsubai range of the Western Ghats mountains, Nashik, (Maharashtra, India).
overview.description: Alang Fort (also known as Alangad or Alang) is a fort located in the Kalsubai range of the Western Ghats mountains, Nashik, (Maharashtra, India). Alang Fort, Madangad Fort, Kulang Fort, and the trek connecting them are known as Alang, Madan, and Kulang (AMK). Alang Fort is regarded as one of the most difficult places to reach in the region. As the Maharashtra Culture Government website notes, "Alang-Madan-Kurland is one of Maharashtra's most challenging treks, especially because of its challenging stretches of water and dense forests." This route is suitable for experienced mountaineers.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Madangad Fort
topPlaces.description: Madangad is a fort in the Nashik region of Maharashtra, India in the Kalsubai range.

topPlaces.name: Ghatghar Dam
topPlaces.description: Ghatghar Dam refers to two associated gravity dams built using roller-compacted concrete, the first use in India. They are situated in Ghatghar village, in Ahmednagar district Maharashtra, India.

topPlaces.name: Kalsubai
topPlaces.description: Kalsubai is a mountain in the Western Ghats, located in the Indian state of Maharashtra. Kalsubai is the highest peak in Maharashtra and is located in Ahilyanagar district in Akole Taluka.

topPlaces.name: Bham Dam
topPlaces.description: Bham dam is an earthfill gravity dam on the Bham tributary of Godavari river in Nashik district in the State of Maharashtra in India.

topPlaces.name: Bhavali Dam
topPlaces.description: Bhavali Dam, is an earthfill dam on Bham river near Igatpuri, Nashik district in state of Maharashtra in India. Bhavali Dam is located near village Bhavali on Darana river tributary of Godavari river.

topPlaces.name: Ratangad
topPlaces.description: Ratangad is a fort in Ratanwadi, Maharashtra, India, overlooking Bhandardara in the Ahmadnagar district of Maharashtra bordering the Thane district. The fort is about 400 years old.

topPlaces.name: Amruteshwar Temple
topPlaces.description: Amruteshwar Temple is an intricately carved Shiva temple in Ratanwadi. It is over 1200 years old and was built by the rulers of Shilahara dynasty in the 9th century CE. This is one of twelve Shiva temples built by King Jhanjha.

topPlaces.name: Ratanwadi
topPlaces.description: Ratanwadi is a village in Ahmednagar district, Maharashtra, India.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Alang Fort?
faq.a: The best time to visit Alang Fort is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Alang Fort?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Shirdi Airport (76 km) and the nearest railway station is Nashik Road (45 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Alang Fort?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Alang Fort at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Alang Fort?
faq.a: Stays in Alang Fort start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Alang Fort?
faq.a: The top attractions are Madangad Fort, Ghatghar Dam, Kalsubai, Bham Dam, Bhavali Dam.
